findings.summary: Tool.Def is the core interface with id, description, parameters, execute, jsonSchema, cacheable, formatValidationError. Tool.define() returns an Effect<Info> where Info.init() is a lazy wrapper that compiles the schema parser and wires caching/truncation/tracing.

ToolRegistry is an Effect service with 4 methods: ids, all, named, tools. It initializes all 58 built-in tools in an Effect.all block, filters by runtime flags and model, and merges custom/plugin tools. Plugin tools are wrapped at the boundary via fromPlugin().

SessionTools.resolve() is the AI SDK bridge: it iterates registry.tools() items, converts each Tool.Def to an AI SDK tool() object with jsonSchema, execute wrapper that runs inside EffectBridge + semaphore, publishing start/completed/failed bus events.
ToolCache provides getOrCompute with inflight dedup via Deferred, and invalidate(). No TTL. Cache key is `${id}:${JSON.stringify(decoded)}`.
Thin lifecycle event publisher: publishStarted/publishCompleted/publishFailed → CoordEvents.SubagentPhaseChanged via Bus.
ToolJsonSchema.fromTool() converts Effect Schema.Top to JSONSchema7 with normalization (strip null, flatten anyOf, bound integers, inline $ref). Cached via WeakMap.
59 tool files all follow same pattern: Tool.define(id, Effect.gen init), export const XxxTool, optional export * as Xxx. Cacheable only on read-only pure tools. All end with .pipe(Effect.orDie).
